# Memory index

Repo-resident agent memory (ADR-CORE-003). Generated by `scripts/sync-index.mjs`; see
[README.md](README.md) for what belongs here.

- [Domain glossary](glossary.md) — Shell terms: HUD design system, HudPanel, capability, IPC command, DTO/bindings, ring buffer, build channel, governance index.
- [Every IPC command logs its result via Serialize for AppError](logging-contract.md) — rule:logging needs action + result logs; central chokepoint is Serialize for AppError which tracing::error!s every IPC error — never bypass it.
- [Open follow-up work on the shell](open-work-backlog.md) — Backlog: no macOS signing secrets; a second, non-Tauri consumer would be the first real proof the core is portable. The cascade itself is live and green.
- [saga-rust-template scope summary](project-scope.md) — A governed, reusable Tauri 2 + React HUD desktop shell. It owns the 'app' layer, consumes the agnostic core from althing, and publishes both to its forks.
- [Memory lives in the repo, not in any per-user Claude folder](repo-is-memory-home.md) — ADR-CORE-003 overrides the session 'auto memory' default: never write to C:\Users\…\.claude\projects — only to the repo's .claude/memory/.
- [Show plan + loaded governance before each task](response-prelude.md) — Open non-trivial tasks with a short prelude — what I'll do + which ADRs/rules/memory I'm consulting — so the governance footprint is auditable per-step.
- [Maintainer conventions](user-conventions.md) — Code/comments/commits in English; Conventional Commits; best-solution-only and verify-first are hard rules; everything built in one pass, no stubs/optional.
- [Bumps follow the work; releases follow the maintainer](version-bump-trigger.md) — Bump SemVer on every landing change (npm version <x> --no-git-tag-version, in the same commit); never tag or release unless the maintainer says so.
